title: The Ascent Without Report

features.name: the-fence-line
features.desc-on-touch: A plantation of marked trees, each one identical to the last. They spiral upward in rows that follow no direction the compass knows. Some climbers never descended.

features.name: care-refusal
features.desc-on-touch: A place where rescue operations stopped. The archive honours this—the deepest care is knowing when to let the climb continue alone.

features.name: last-message-tree
features.desc-on-touch: A carved report that ends mid-thought. The archive completed it once; the completion was filed as corruption. Now it keeps both versions.

features.name: ascending-silent-path
features.desc-on-touch: Marked at intervals by stones that say nothing. You climb through a catalogue of unmarked events. Some archives are kept by refusing to explain them.

features.name: the-height-nobody-reached
features.desc-on-touch: The place above where the climbs end. Archive records it with the same detail as everything else. The absence is as catalogued as any arrival.

inscription: the archive keeps the climb and the climbers equally, whether they ascend or descend or simply stop

file.name: Expedition_Logs_Fragment_1962_2019.txt
file.note: Twelve attempts to reach the ridge. Each one stops at the last written entry. The archive holds all twelve stops with equal care.

exits.label: back down into the light that never reaches here
further up, where no path has reported back
lateral traverse toward the other ascending trees
